title: Implement published-prior-art burst fusion pipeline
summary: Build an original, documented burst alignment, rejection, merge and tone pipeline using lawful publications/data and native Agent OS captures.

title: Optimize low-light noise, detail, color, and motion behavior
summary: Improve low-light output through sensor-aware denoise, temporal fusion, color/tone and capture policy while controlling ghosting and latency.

title: Optimize HDR and local tone mapping
summary: Extend usable dynamic range while preserving color, local contrast, natural highlights, faces and temporal stability.

title: Evaluate optional ML imaging components under bounded policy
summary: Assess whether segmentation, denoise, super-resolution, scene understanding or auto-edit models add measurable value with acceptable provenance, privacy, size, power and fallback.

title: Prove camera API portability across two pipelines
summary: Run the same capture application and conformance suite over two materially different sensor/ISP/software backends.

title: Characterize and budget camera power, memory, bandwidth, and thermal cost
summary: Measure preview/capture/video/computational workloads and enforce quality degradation strategies compatible with mobile thermal and battery limits.

title: Issue controlled proprietary camera-module/ISP RFI
summary: Test whether a vendor can supply quality hardware and documentation/support under replaceable portable contracts without unacceptable cost or lock-in.

title: Evaluate vendor camera path against native reference
summary: Compare selected vendor module/ISP/backend quality, power, latency, control, security, rights, support and replacement cost to native documented pipelines.

title: Select camera route for quality-device track
summary: Choose documented native, controlled proprietary, hybrid or deferred camera route based on measured quality, resources, rights, cost and lifecycle.

title: Bring up a documented i.MX 8M Plus RAW and metadata bench
summary: Bring up a documented i.MX 8M Plus RAW and metadata bench

title: Establish a calibrated camera quality measurement laboratory
summary: Establish a calibrated camera quality measurement laboratory

title: Evaluate industrial and UVC camera modules as compromise reducers
summary: Evaluate industrial and UVC camera modules as compromise reducers

title: Cellular and Telephony Program epic
summary: Implement native cellular services and establish partner/certification routes.

title: Shortlist documented cellular modules and evaluation kits
summary: Identify modules with usable MBIM/QMI/AT documentation, firmware/update support, USB/PCIe, regional bands, certification, lifecycle and vendor FAE access.

title: Commission lawful cellular test environment
summary: Provide shielded/controlled or operator-approved test options, SIMs/accounts, traffic capture, emergency-call safeguards, RF/power tools and data-handling rules.

title: Implement native USB/serial/AT modem transport
summary: Communicate with documented module control/data endpoints through native USB/serial services with framing, timeouts, reset and diagnostics.

title: Implement native MBIM/QMI transport and control proof
summary: Control at least one documented modem protocol and expose portable registration/session operations without a Linux runtime dependency.

title: Implement modem registration and lifecycle state machine
summary: Manage power, initialization, SIM state, network scan/selection, registration, roaming, loss, retry, reset, firmware status and diagnostics explicitly.

title: Implement physical SIM access and secret boundary
summary: Expose SIM presence, PIN/PUK, subscriber/application state and approved operations without leaking credentials or raw APDU authority to ordinary clients.

title: Deliver native cellular data and SMS prototype
summary: Register a module, establish packet data, route network service, send/receive SMS, persist receipts, and recover from loss/reset.

title: Harden cellular recovery, suspend, roaming, and power behavior
summary: Make connectivity resilient and bounded across coverage changes, modem resets, SIM changes, suspend/wake, roaming policy and data limits.

title: Model and evaluate eSIM lifecycle and partner requirements
summary: Define eUICC discovery, profile download/enable/disable/delete, credentials, user consent, recovery, device migration and GSMA/vendor/operator dependencies.

title: Integrate modem-assisted GNSS where supportable
summary: Expose location/time observations from a supported modem or separate GNSS path with permissions, accuracy, assistance-data, offline and power semantics.

title: Define emergency-calling safety boundary before voice work
summary: Prevent misleading or accidental emergency behavior and specify requirements, test methods, UI, fallback, location and regulatory/operator dependencies.

title: Establish native voice and IMS feasibility
summary: Determine whether vendor/carrier-supported voice, IMS registration, audio routing, SMS over IMS, provisioning, handover and emergency behavior can be implemented and maintained.

title: Implement telephony audio-routing contract and test path
summary: Route microphone, speaker/earpiece, Bluetooth/USB, modem/IMS streams, tones, mute, volume and privacy through native audio policy.

title: Build cellular certification and carrier engagement plan
summary: Map module/final-device approvals, PTCRB/GCF, operator acceptance, IMS profiles, emergency, RF/SAR, OTA, variants, updates and regression responsibility.

title: Secure cellular module vendor and FAE relationships
summary: Obtain documentation, firmware/update policy, samples, support, certification data and a route for Linux-independent native integration.

title: Integrate cellular data/SMS into open-phone product experience
summary: Expose honest signal/registration/data/SMS/SIM/roaming/usage state and actions in the entity shell with recovery and migration behavior.

title: Decide connected-device versus full-phone product route
summary: Select supported cellular claims and investment based on module, data/SMS, eSIM, voice/IMS, emergency, certification, cost and partner evidence.

title: Verify Pixel 9 acquisition, unlock, relock, and stock restore
summary: Acquire appropriate non-carrier-restricted development units and prove a reversible documented unlock/recovery path before destructive research.

title: Automate Pixel stock recovery and artifact inventory
summary: Create safe repeatable stock-image restoration, build/firmware identification, partition inventory, boot-state capture and device reset procedures.

title: Enforce Pixel Android/Linux dependency containment
summary: Continuously prove that Pixel-only stock, sidecar, trace, firmware and bring-up aids do not enter native portable code, IDL, schemas or product state.

title: Build Pixel 9 stock camera/display/power quality oracle
summary: Capture repeatable stock baselines and test procedures for image/video, display/frame/input, battery/thermal/suspend and common workflows.
